Security review completed
Reviewed by product owner

features.title: Role-Based Access Control Configuration
features.description: Implement RBAC across the platform with predefined roles (Technician, Dispatcher, Supervisor, Finance, Account Manager, IT Admin) that restrict access to data and features based on job function.
acceptance_criteria: Given a user with the Technician role, when they access the platform, then they can only view their assigned work orders and cannot see customer contract data
Given a user with the Account Manager role, when they access customer data, then they can view sensitive contract information
Given an IT admin, when they manage role assignments, then they can assign and revoke roles through an admin interface synced with Azure AD groups
definition_of_done: All six predefined roles configured and tested
Role assignments sync with Azure AD security groups
Unauthorized access attempts logged for audit
Penetration testing completed for role boundary enforcement
Reviewed by product owner

features.title: Sensitive Data Access Restriction
features.description: Implement fine-grained data access controls ensuring sensitive customer contract data is only visible to users with Account Manager role or above, with all access attempts logged.
acceptance_criteria: Given a user without Account Manager or higher role, when they attempt to access customer contract data, then the data is hidden and access is denied
Given an Account Manager, when they view a customer profile, then contract details including pricing and terms are visible
Given any attempt to access restricted data, when the attempt occurs, then it is logged with user identity and outcome (allowed/denied)
definition_of_done: Data classification labels applied to all sensitive fields
Access control tested for all six roles
Audit logging verified for both allowed and denied access
Reviewed by product owner

features.title: Compliance Document Capture & Storage
features.description: Implement secure storage for all compliance documentation captured on-site (photos, digital sign-offs, completed checklists) with metadata tagging, encryption at rest, and linkage to the originating work order.
acceptance_criteria: Given a technician submits a work order with compliance documents, when the documents are stored, then each document is encrypted at rest and tagged with work order ID, timestamp, and technician ID
Given all customer data must reside in the US, when compliance documents are stored, then the storage account is verified to be in a US Azure region
Given a compliance document is uploaded, when it is saved, then it is immutable and cannot be modified or deleted by non-admin users
definition_of_done: Storage uses Azure Blob Storage with US-region constraint
Encryption at rest enabled and verified
Immutability policies configured
Storage tested with large photo files (up to 20MB each)
Reviewed by product owner

features.title: Seven-Year Retention & Audit Trail
features.description: Configure document retention policies ensuring all compliance records are retained for a minimum of seven years with a complete audit trail tracking who created, accessed, or modified each document.
acceptance_criteria: Given a compliance document stored in the system, when the retention period is checked, then it is set to a minimum of seven years from the creation date
Given any user accesses a compliance document, when the access occurs, then an audit log entry is created with user ID, timestamp, and action type
Given an IT admin queries the audit trail, when they search by work order or date range, then all access and modification events are returned
definition_of_done: Retention policies automated via Azure Storage lifecycle rules
Audit logs stored separately and tamper-proof
Retention compliance verified with sample data across 7-year simulated timeline
Reviewed by product owner

features.title: Teams Work Order Notifications
features.description: Integrate with Microsoft Teams to send work order notifications (new assignment, status changes, approval requests) directly to technicians and supervisors via Teams messages and adaptive cards.
acceptance_criteria: Given a technician is assigned a new work order, when the assignment is saved, then they receive a Teams notification with an adaptive card showing work order summary
Given a supervisor has a pending approval, when the work order enters the approval queue, then they receive a Teams notification with approve/reject action buttons
Given a technician is using Teams on iOS or Android, when they receive a notification, then the adaptive card renders correctly on the mobile Teams client
definition_of_done: Teams bot/connector registered and configured
Adaptive cards tested on desktop and mobile Teams clients
Notification delivery latency under 60 seconds
Reviewed by product owner

features.title: Teams Work Order Interaction
features.description: Enable technicians to view work order details, update status, and perform basic actions (accept, flag, mark en route) directly from within Microsoft Teams without switching to the dedicated mobile app.
acceptance_criteria: Given a technician receives a work order notification in Teams, when they click 'View Details', then full work order information is displayed in a Teams task module
Given a technician viewing a work order in Teams, when they tap 'Accept' or 'Mark En Route', then the work order status updates in the platform in real time
Given a technician with no connectivity, when they attempt an action in Teams, then the action is queued and a message indicates it will be processed when connectivity returns
definition_of_done: Teams task modules render work order details correctly
Status update actions sync with the platform backend
Tested on iOS and Android Teams clients
No additional app installation required beyond Teams
Reviewed by product owner

features.title: Azure Infrastructure Provisioning & US Data Residency
features.description: Provision the Azure-native hosting infrastructure in US regions, configure networking, storage, and compute resources, and enforce data residency policies ensuring all customer data remains within the United States.
acceptance_criteria: Given the infrastructure is provisioned, when Azure resource locations are audited, then all resources hosting customer data are in US Azure regions
Given a deployment attempt to a non-US region, when the deployment runs, then Azure Policy denies the deployment and logs the violation
Given the infrastructure requirements, when the environment is provisioned, then it is defined as Infrastructure as Code (Bicep/Terraform) for reproducibility
definition_of_done: Infrastructure as Code templates created and version controlled
Azure Policy for US-only data residency enforced
Network security groups and firewall rules configured
Cost estimate documented and approved
Reviewed by product owner
features.specTitle: Azure Infrastructure Provisioning & US Data Residency

features.title: Phased Rollout & Parallel Operation Support
features.description: Implement the phased rollout strategy enabling the new platform to run in parallel with the legacy system during the transition period, including data synchronization between old and new systems and a clear cutover plan.
acceptance_criteria: Given the pilot region is identified, when the new platform is deployed, then both legacy and new systems operate simultaneously for the pilot group
Given work orders are created in either system during parallel operation, when data is synced, then both systems reflect consistent work order status
Given the pilot validation is complete, when the cutover decision is made, then a documented runbook guides the transition from legacy to new platform
definition_of_done: Parallel operation tested with pilot region data
Data sync between legacy and new systems validated
Cutover runbook documented and reviewed
Rollback plan documented
Reviewed by product owner

features.title: SAP S/4HANA API Integration Layer
features.description: Build the modern API integration layer replacing legacy stored procedures for SAP S/4HANA connectivity, supporting financials, inventory, and invoicing data exchange with versioned APIs resilient to SAP updates.
acceptance_criteria: Given the integration layer is deployed, when the platform sends financial data to SAP, then invoices are created correctly in SAP S/4HANA
Given SAP performs a quarterly update, when the integration is tested, then all API contracts remain functional due to versioning
Given an API call fails, when the integration layer retries, then it follows an exponential backoff strategy and logs the failure for IT monitoring
definition_of_done: All legacy stored procedures replaced with RESTful API calls
API versioning implemented (v1 baseline)
Retry and circuit breaker patterns implemented
Integration tested against SAP sandbox
Comprehensive API documentation published
Reviewed by product owner

features.title: API Documentation & Developer Portal
features.description: Create comprehensive API documentation for all custom-built platform components, published through a developer portal that enables internal teams to understand, test, and integrate with the platform APIs.
acceptance_criteria: Given a developer accessing the API portal, when they browse available endpoints, then they see complete request/response schemas with examples for every custom API
Given API documentation, when a developer reads an endpoint description, then they can successfully make a test call using the provided examples
Given a new API endpoint is deployed, when the deployment completes, then the documentation is automatically updated via the CI/CD pipeline
definition_of_done: OpenAPI/Swagger specifications generated for all custom APIs
Developer portal hosted and accessible to internal teams
Auto-generation integrated into CI/CD pipeline
Documentation reviewed by development team for accuracy
Reviewed by product owner

userStories.title: Set up cross-platform mobile app framework
userStories.user_story: As a field technician, I want to install and launch a mobile app on my iOS or Android device, so that I can access the field service platform from any supported phone or tablet.
acceptance_criteria: Given a technician with an iOS 16+ device, when they install the app from the App Store, then it installs and launches successfully within 3 seconds cold start
Given a technician with an Android 13+ device, when they install the app from the Play Store, then it installs and launches successfully within 3 seconds cold start
Given the app is launched for the first time, when the technician reaches the login screen, then it displays the Azure AD SSO login option
tasks: Set up cross-platform project scaffold (React Native or .NET MAUI)
Configure iOS and Android build pipelines
Implement splash screen and initial loading flow
Create app store distribution profiles and signing certificates
Write unit tests for app initialization
userStories.notes: Framework choice (React Native vs .NET MAUI) should be finalized in architecture review. Cold start target is < 3 seconds per NFR.

userStories.title: Implement mobile app navigation and home screen
userStories.user_story: As a field technician, I want a clear home screen with intuitive navigation, so that I can access all primary functions within two taps.
acceptance_criteria: Given an authenticated technician, when they view the home screen, then they see tiles or tabs for My Work Orders, Schedule, Parts, and Notifications
Given any primary function, when the technician navigates from the home screen, then the function is reachable within two taps
Given the technician is on any sub-screen, when they tap the back or home button, then they return to the previous screen or home screen without data loss
tasks: Design navigation architecture (tab bar or drawer)
Implement home screen layout with primary function tiles
Build navigation stack with back/home support
Conduct accessibility audit (WCAG 2.1 AA compliance)
Write UI tests for navigation flows
userStories.notes: Navigation pattern should be validated with 3+ field technicians in usability testing.

userStories.title: Implement responsive layout for phones and tablets
userStories.user_story: As a field technician, I want the app to adapt its layout to my device form factor, so that I can use it effectively on both my phone and a ruggedized tablet.
acceptance_criteria: Given a technician using a phone (< 7 inch screen), when they view any screen, then the layout uses a single-column design optimized for one-handed use
Given a technician using a tablet (≥ 7 inch screen), when they view work order details, then the layout uses a split-pane design showing the list and detail side by side
Given a device orientation change, when the technician rotates the device, then the layout adapts without losing form state or scroll position
tasks: Define responsive breakpoints for phone and tablet layouts
Implement adaptive layout components (single-column and split-pane)
Handle orientation changes with state preservation
Test on Surface Pro, iPhone 14, and Samsung Galaxy S23 form factors
Write snapshot tests for layout variants
userStories.notes: Surface Pro ruggedized tablets are under consideration for the standardized device program.

userStories.title: Implement local offline data storage
userStories.user_story: As a field technician, I want my work order data stored locally on my device, so that I can view and complete work orders even when I have no network connectivity.
acceptance_criteria: Given a technician in an area with no connectivity, when they open the app, then all previously synced work orders are available for viewing
Given the device has no connectivity, when the technician completes a work order and saves it, then the data is persisted to the local database
Given the local database contains work order data, when the technician views photos and attachments, then cached attachments are available offline
tasks: Select and configure local database (SQLite or Realm)
Define offline data schema mirroring server-side work order model
Implement repository pattern with local-first data access
Build storage management for photos and large attachments
Write integration tests for offline CRUD operations
userStories.notes: Local storage must handle photos up to 20MB each. Estimated capacity planning needed for 200 concurrent devices.

userStories.title: Build background data sync engine
userStories.user_story: As a field technician, I want my offline changes to automatically sync when connectivity is restored, so that the back office sees my updates without me taking manual action.
acceptance_criteria: Given a technician whose device regains connectivity, when the sync engine detects a network, then all locally queued changes are uploaded to the server within 60 seconds
Given multiple work orders were updated offline, when the sync runs, then each work order is synced independently so a failure in one does not block others
Given a sync is in progress, when the technician loses connectivity again, then the sync pauses gracefully and resumes when the connection returns
tasks: Implement network connectivity detection service
Build sync queue with per-record tracking and retry logic
Implement background sync using platform-specific APIs (iOS BackgroundTasks, Android WorkManager)
Add sync status indicator to the mobile UI
Load test with 200 concurrent sync operations
userStories.notes: Must be resilient to intermittent connectivity (rural job sites). Load testing with 200 concurrent syncs is part of the definition of done.

userStories.title: Implement conflict resolution for offline edits
userStories.user_story: As a field technician, I want the system to handle conflicts when two people edit the same work order offline, so that no data is lost and I am notified of any merge issues.
acceptance_criteria: Given two users edited the same work order field offline, when both sync, then the system applies last-write-wins for non-critical fields and flags critical fields for manual review
Given a conflict is detected during sync, when the technician opens the app, then they see a notification identifying the conflicting work order and fields
Given a conflict is flagged for manual review, when the supervisor reviews it, then they can choose which version to keep or merge values
tasks: Define conflict resolution rules (last-write-wins vs. manual review per field)
Implement vector clock or timestamp-based conflict detection
Build conflict notification UI for affected users
Create supervisor conflict resolution screen
Write unit tests for all conflict scenarios
userStories.notes: Conflict resolution rules should be documented and approved by the product owner before implementation.

userStories.title: View assigned work orders on mobile
userStories.user_story: As a field technician, I want to view my assigned work orders on my mobile device, so that I can see my schedule and job details while on the road.
acceptance_criteria: Given a technician with assigned work orders, when they open the My Work Orders screen, then they see a list of all assigned work orders sorted by scheduled date
Given a work order in the list, when the technician taps it, then they see full details including customer info, asset info, tasks, and parts required
Given the technician is offline, when they view assigned work orders, then all previously synced work orders display with a visual indicator showing the data freshness
tasks: Build work order list screen with sorting and grouping
Implement work order detail screen with all required fields
Integrate with local offline data store for offline access
Add data freshness indicator (last synced timestamp)
Write UI tests for list and detail screens

userStories.title: Complete and submit work orders on mobile
userStories.user_story: As a field technician, I want to fill out and submit completed work orders from my mobile device, so that the back office receives my updates immediately without paper forms.
acceptance_criteria: Given a technician on-site with an open work order, when they complete all required fields and tap Submit, then the work order status changes to Completed and enters the approval queue
Given a technician attempts to submit an incomplete form, when required fields are missing, then the form highlights missing fields and prevents submission
Given the technician is offline, when they submit a completed work order, then it is saved locally and marked for sync when connectivity is restored
tasks: Build work order completion form with all required fields
Implement client-side form validation for required fields
Wire submission to approval workflow via API (or offline queue)
Add submission confirmation screen with summary
Write end-to-end tests for online and offline submission paths

userStories.title: Capture and attach photos to work orders
userStories.user_story: As a field technician, I want to take photos on-site and attach them to a work order, so that visual documentation of the job is captured digitally.
acceptance_criteria: Given a technician on-site, when they tap the photo capture button on a work order, then the device camera opens and the captured photo is attached to the work order
Given multiple photos are needed, when the technician captures additional photos, then all photos are added to the work order as a scrollable gallery
Given the technician is offline, when they capture photos, then the photos are stored locally and sync with the work order when connectivity returns
tasks: Implement camera integration using platform-native APIs
Build photo gallery view within the work order detail screen
Compress photos for optimal storage (configurable quality)
Store photos in local offline database with work order linkage
Write integration tests for camera capture flow
userStories.notes: Photos may be up to 20MB each. Compression strategy should balance quality with storage/bandwidth.

userStories.title: Collect digital signatures from customers
userStories.user_story: As a field technician, I want to collect a digital signature from the customer on-site, so that the job sign-off is captured without paper forms.
acceptance_criteria: Given a technician has completed a job, when they tap Request Sign-Off, then a digital signature pad is displayed for the customer
Given the customer draws their signature, when they confirm, then the signature image is stored and linked to the work order with a timestamp
Given the technician is offline, when a signature is captured, then it is stored locally and synced when connectivity returns
tasks: Implement touch-based signature capture component
Store signatures as PNG images with metadata (timestamp, work order ID)
Integrate signature capture into work order completion flow
Test on both phone and tablet form factors
Write unit tests for signature storage and retrieval

userStories.title: Complete compliance checklists on mobile
userStories.user_story: As a field technician, I want to complete compliance checklists on my mobile device during on-site work, so that all regulatory requirements are documented digitally.
acceptance_criteria: Given a work order with compliance requirements, when the technician opens the checklist tab, then all required checklist items are displayed
Given a checklist with mandatory items, when the technician attempts to submit without completing all items, then the form prevents submission and highlights incomplete items
Given the technician is offline, when they complete a checklist, then it is saved locally and synced with the work order when connectivity returns
tasks: Build configurable checklist component (checkbox, text, photo-required items)
Implement checklist template system per work order type
Integrate checklists into work order completion flow as a required step
Support offline checklist completion and sync
Write tests for checklist validation logic
userStories.notes: Checklist templates must be configurable per work order type — admin configuration UI may be a separate story.

userStories.title: Create work orders from customer calls
userStories.user_story: As a dispatcher, I want to create a new work order when a customer calls in, so that their service request is captured and enters the dispatch queue.
acceptance_criteria: Given a dispatcher receiving a customer call, when they complete the new work order form with customer, location, issue description, and priority, then a work order is created and enters the unassigned queue
Given the dispatcher selects a customer, when the customer is chosen, then the form auto-populates with customer address and contact details from the CRM
Given required fields are not completed, when the dispatcher tries to save, then validation errors are shown and the form is not submitted
tasks: Design and build the new work order entry form (web)
Implement customer lookup with auto-population of details
Create work order API endpoint for manual creation
Add the new work order to the dispatch queue
Write unit and integration tests

userStories.title: Auto-create work orders from maintenance contracts
userStories.user_story: As a dispatcher, I want work orders to be automatically created when maintenance contract triggers fire, so that scheduled maintenance is dispatched without manual intervention.
acceptance_criteria: Given an active maintenance contract with a scheduled trigger, when the trigger date arrives, then a work order is automatically created and placed in the dispatch queue
Given an auto-created work order, when a dispatcher views it, then it is clearly labeled as contract-triggered with the contract reference linked
Given a contract trigger fires but required data is incomplete, when the system processes it, then the work order is created in Draft status and the dispatcher is alerted
tasks: Build scheduled job to evaluate contract trigger dates daily
Implement auto-creation logic with contract data mapping
Label auto-created work orders with trigger source metadata
Add alerting for draft work orders needing dispatcher attention
Write integration tests for trigger evaluation and work order creation
userStories.notes: The architecture should be extensible for IoT sensor triggers in Phase 2.

userStories.title: Search and link Maximo assets to work orders
userStories.user_story: As a dispatcher, I want to search for customer assets in IBM Maximo and link them to a work order, so that technicians know exactly which equipment they are servicing.
acceptance_criteria: Given a dispatcher creating or editing a work order, when they search for an asset by name or ID, then matching assets from IBM Maximo are displayed with key details (asset ID, type, location)
Given search results are displayed, when the dispatcher selects an asset, then it is linked to the work order and the asset details are saved
Given the Maximo API is temporarily unavailable, when a search is attempted, then the system displays a graceful error message and allows the work order to be saved without an asset link
tasks: Build Maximo API client for asset search endpoint
Implement asset search UI component with type-ahead
Store asset linkage on work order record
Implement error handling for Maximo API unavailability
Write integration tests with mock Maximo responses
userStories.notes: Asset search must return results within 3 seconds per feature acceptance criteria.

userStories.title: View asset maintenance history from work orders
userStories.user_story: As a field technician, I want to view the maintenance history and specifications for a linked asset, so that I have full context before starting a repair.
acceptance_criteria: Given a work order linked to a Maximo asset, when the technician taps View Asset Details, then the asset's maintenance history and specifications are displayed
Given the technician is offline, when they view asset details, then the last cached asset data is shown with an indicator of when it was last refreshed
Given an asset has no maintenance history, when the technician views it, then a clear message indicates no prior history and core specifications are still displayed
tasks: Build asset detail screen in the mobile app
Fetch and display maintenance history from Maximo API
Cache asset details for offline access
Display data freshness indicator
Write UI and integration tests

userStories.title: Supervisor approval of completed work orders
userStories.user_story: As a supervisor, I want to review and approve or reject completed work orders in an approval queue, so that work quality is verified before finance review.
acceptance_criteria: Given a technician has submitted a completed work order, when the supervisor opens their approval queue, then the work order appears with all completion details including photos and checklists
Given a supervisor reviews a work order, when they tap Approve, then the work order advances to the finance review stage and the technician is notified
Given a supervisor finds issues, when they tap Reject with comments, then the work order is returned to the technician with the rejection reason and a notification is sent
tasks: Build supervisor approval queue UI (web and/or mobile)
Implement approve/reject API endpoints with workflow state transitions
Send notifications on approval or rejection (in-app and Teams)
Capture approval timestamp and supervisor identity for audit trail
Write end-to-end tests for approval and rejection flows

userStories.title: Finance review and approval before invoicing
userStories.user_story: As a finance reviewer, I want to review supervisor-approved work orders with cost details before they are sent to SAP for invoicing, so that billing accuracy is ensured.
acceptance_criteria: Given a supervisor has approved a work order, when finance opens their review queue, then the work order appears with cost breakdown, labor hours, and parts used
Given a finance reviewer approves a work order, when they confirm, then the work order is marked as ready for invoicing and the SAP invoicing process is triggered
Given a finance reviewer rejects a work order, when they submit with comments, then the work order returns to the supervisor stage with the rejection reason
tasks: Build finance review queue UI with cost details view
Implement finance approve/reject API with state transitions
Trigger SAP invoicing process on finance approval
Capture audit trail for finance review actions
Write integration tests for end-to-end approval chain

userStories.title: Map legacy paper forms to digital equivalents
userStories.user_story: As a field technician, I want digital work order forms that capture the same fields as the current paper forms, so that I have a familiar experience without any data being lost in the transition.
acceptance_criteria: Given the current paper work order form, when compared field-by-field to the digital form, then all data fields from the paper version are present in the digital version
Given a technician filling the digital form, when they review the layout, then the fields are logically grouped and labeled using familiar terminology
Given the digital form captures all required fields, when the data is submitted, then it flows directly into the approval workflow without manual re-entry
tasks: Audit all existing paper form fields and create a mapping document
Design digital form layout with logical field grouping
Implement all mapped fields in the mobile work order form
Validate mapping completeness with Priya Nair (BA)
Write tests verifying all fields are persisted

userStories.title: Enforce required field validation on digital forms
userStories.user_story: As a dispatcher, I want the digital work order forms to enforce required field completion, so that incomplete data never enters the approval workflow.
acceptance_criteria: Given a technician filling out a digital form, when they miss a required field and attempt to submit, then the form highlights the missing field with a clear error message
Given a form with conditional required fields (e.g., parts used requires quantity), when a triggering field is filled, then dependent required fields become enforced
Given all required fields are completed, when the technician submits, then the form processes successfully without validation errors
tasks: Define required field rules for each work order type
Implement client-side validation with inline error messages
Implement conditional field requirement logic
Write unit tests for all validation rules

userStories.title: Display unassigned work orders on dispatch board
userStories.user_story: As a dispatcher, I want to see all unassigned work orders on a digital dispatch board, so that I can manage the daily schedule without a physical whiteboard.
acceptance_criteria: Given work orders exist in the unassigned queue, when the dispatcher opens the dispatch board, then all unassigned work orders are displayed with priority, customer, and location
Given the dispatch board is open, when a new work order is created, then it appears on the board within 60 seconds without a manual refresh
Given the dispatch board has many work orders, when the dispatcher filters by priority or region, then the list updates immediately to show matching work orders
tasks: Design dispatch board layout with unassigned work order panel
Implement real-time data updates via WebSocket or SignalR
Build filter/sort controls for priority, region, and date
Ensure board loads within 2 seconds with a full day's data
Write UI and performance tests
userStories.notes: Board must load within 2 seconds per feature DoD.

userStories.title: Drag-and-drop work order assignment
userStories.user_story: As a dispatcher, I want to drag and drop work orders onto technician time slots, so that I can assign jobs quickly and intuitively.
acceptance_criteria: Given an unassigned work order on the dispatch board, when the dispatcher drags it onto a technician's time slot, then the work order is assigned to that technician for the selected time
Given a technician's schedule is full, when the dispatcher attempts to drop a work order, then a visual indicator warns of the scheduling conflict
Given a work order is assigned via drag-and-drop, when the assignment is saved, then the technician receives a notification of the new assignment
tasks: Implement drag-and-drop interaction library for the scheduling grid
Build technician schedule timeline view with available slots
Add conflict detection for overlapping assignments
Trigger assignment notification on successful drop
Test drag-and-drop on Chrome and Edge browsers
userStories.notes: Usability testing with 3+ dispatchers required per feature DoD.

userStories.title: Suggest optimal technician assignments
userStories.user_story: As a dispatcher, I want the system to suggest the best technician for a work order based on location and skills, so that I can make faster and smarter dispatch decisions.
acceptance_criteria: Given an unassigned work order, when the dispatcher clicks Suggest Assignment, then the system ranks available technicians by proximity and relevant skills
Given multiple technicians are equally close, when the suggestion is generated, then the system considers current workload and availability to break the tie
Given no technicians are available in the optimal time window, when the dispatcher views suggestions, then the system recommends the next available slot with the best-fit technician
tasks: Build assignment optimization algorithm considering location, skills, and workload
Integrate technician location data from FleetComplete for proximity scoring
Implement skills matching against work order requirements
Design suggestion UI with ranked technician list and rationale
Write tests for optimization scoring logic
userStories.notes: Initial version uses basic distance + skill matching. ML-based optimization could be a Phase 2 enhancement.

userStories.title: Display real-time technician locations on map
userStories.user_story: As a dispatcher, I want to see real-time technician locations on a map within the dispatch board, so that I can assign the nearest available technician to urgent jobs.
acceptance_criteria: Given the dispatch board is open, when the dispatcher toggles the map view, then all active technician locations are shown as markers with names and current status
Given a technician's vehicle is moving, when the dispatcher views the map, then the marker position updates within 30 seconds
Given 40+ technicians are active (pilot region), when the map renders, then all markers display correctly without performance degradation
tasks: Integrate FleetComplete GPS API for real-time location data
Implement map component with technician markers (Azure Maps or Bing Maps)
Build polling or WebSocket connection for location updates
Add technician status tooltips on marker hover
Performance test with 40+ simultaneous markers
userStories.notes: API rate limits for FleetComplete must be documented and respected.

userStories.title: Handle stale GPS data gracefully
userStories.user_story: As a dispatcher, I want to be notified when technician location data is stale or unavailable, so that I do not make dispatch decisions based on outdated information.
acceptance_criteria: Given the FleetComplete API is unavailable, when the dispatcher views the map, then a banner indicates location data may be stale and shows the last successful update time
Given a technician's GPS has not updated in more than 5 minutes, when the dispatcher views the map, then that technician's marker is visually distinguished (e.g., grayed out) with last known time
Given the FleetComplete API recovers, when the connection is restored, then the map resumes live updates and the stale data banner is removed
tasks: Implement health check for FleetComplete API connectivity
Add visual indicators for stale markers (grayed out, timestamp badge)
Display banner notification for API outages
Implement automatic recovery detection and banner removal
Write tests for degraded and recovery scenarios

userStories.title: Send push notifications for new assignments
userStories.user_story: As a field technician, I want to receive a push notification when I am assigned a new work order, so that I am immediately aware of new jobs without checking the app.
acceptance_criteria: Given a dispatcher assigns a work order to a technician, when the assignment is saved, then the technician receives a push notification within 60 seconds
Given the technician's device is offline, when the device regains connectivity, then any missed assignment notifications are delivered
Given the notification is received, when the technician taps it, then the app opens directly to the assigned work order detail screen
tasks: Configure push notification service (Azure Notification Hubs or Firebase Cloud Messaging)
Register device tokens during app authentication
Implement server-side notification dispatch on work order assignment
Handle deep linking from notification to work order detail
Test push notifications on iOS and Android

userStories.title: Accept or flag work order assignments
userStories.user_story: As a field technician, I want to accept or flag an assigned work order from my mobile device, so that the dispatcher knows I can take the job or needs to reassign it.
acceptance_criteria: Given a technician receives an assignment notification, when they tap it, then the work order details are displayed with Accept and Flag buttons
Given the technician taps Accept, when the action is confirmed, then the work order status updates to Accepted and the dispatcher sees the confirmation
Given the technician taps Flag, when they provide a reason (e.g., too far, missing skills), then the dispatcher is notified and the work order returns to the unassigned queue
tasks: Build Accept/Flag action buttons on work order detail screen
Implement status update API for accept and flag actions
Notify dispatcher on flag with reason text
Return flagged work orders to unassigned queue
Write end-to-end tests for accept and flag flows

userStories.title: Check parts availability from work order view
userStories.user_story: As a field technician, I want to check real-time parts availability from my work order, so that I know if the required parts are in stock before traveling to the job site.
acceptance_criteria: Given a technician viewing a work order, when they tap Check Parts, then the system queries SAP S/4HANA and displays current stock levels for all required parts
Given a part is in stock, when the results are displayed, then a green indicator shows availability with the quantity on hand
Given the SAP API is slow or unavailable, when a parts check is initiated, then the system times out after 10 seconds and suggests the technician call the warehouse directly
tasks: Build parts availability check UI button on work order detail screen
Implement SAP S/4HANA inventory query API call
Display results with color-coded availability indicators
Implement 10-second timeout with graceful fallback message
Write integration tests with mock SAP responses
userStories.notes: Response time must be under 5 seconds for the 95th percentile per feature DoD.

userStories.title: Display warehouse locations and pickup times
userStories.user_story: As a field technician, I want to see which warehouse has the parts I need and the estimated pickup time, so that I can plan my route efficiently.
acceptance_criteria: Given parts are available at one or more warehouses, when the technician views the parts check results, then each warehouse is listed with its name, address, and distance from the job site
Given a warehouse has the required parts, when the technician views details, then an estimated pickup time is displayed based on warehouse operating hours
Given multiple warehouses have the parts, when the results are shown, then they are sorted by proximity to the technician's current location or the job site
tasks: Extend SAP inventory query to include warehouse location data
Implement proximity sorting using job site coordinates
Display warehouse details (address, hours, distance)
Add map link to warehouse for navigation
Write unit tests for sorting and display logic

userStories.title: Auto-verify parts before dispatch confirmation
userStories.user_story: As a dispatcher, I want the system to automatically check parts availability when I am about to dispatch a work order, so that I avoid sending a technician to a job without the right parts.
acceptance_criteria: Given a dispatcher is about to assign a work order, when the system checks parts availability, then a green/yellow/red indicator shows parts readiness on the dispatch board
Given all required parts are available, when the indicator is green, then the dispatcher can proceed with dispatch without delay
Given the parts check fails due to SAP unavailability, when the system cannot verify, then a yellow warning is shown and the dispatcher can override with a documented justification
tasks: Implement pre-dispatch parts verification trigger on the dispatch board
Build traffic light indicator UI (green/yellow/red)
Integrate with SAP inventory API for automatic verification
Allow dispatcher override with mandatory justification field
Write integration tests for automated verification flow

userStories.title: Alert dispatchers on parts unavailability
userStories.user_story: As a dispatcher, I want to be alerted when required parts are unavailable and see alternatives or restock dates, so that I can reschedule or adjust the job accordingly.
acceptance_criteria: Given required parts are unavailable, when the dispatcher views the alert, then alternative compatible parts or estimated restock dates are suggested
Given a work order has been dispatched with a parts warning, when the technician views the order on mobile, then the parts risk is clearly flagged with details
Given parts unavailability is detected, when the dispatcher chooses to reschedule, then the work order is returned to the queue with a parts-pending status
tasks: Build parts unavailability alert notification for dispatchers
Query SAP for alternative parts and restock estimates
Propagate parts risk flag to mobile work order view
Implement reschedule action with parts-pending status
Write tests for alert and reschedule flows

userStories.title: Replace legacy SAP stored procedures with REST APIs
userStories.user_story: As an IT admin, I want all SAP S/4HANA integrations to use modern REST APIs instead of legacy stored procedures, so that the integration is maintainable and resilient to SAP updates.
acceptance_criteria: Given the legacy stored procedures are identified, when the migration is complete, then all data exchange with SAP uses RESTful API calls instead of stored procedures
Given the new API integration is deployed, when SAP inventory data changes, then the platform reflects the update within 5 minutes
Given a data discrepancy between SAP and the platform, when an admin investigates, then a reconciliation log shows the source and timing of the discrepancy
tasks: Audit all existing SAP stored procedures and document their function
Map each stored procedure to the equivalent SAP S/4HANA REST API
Implement REST API clients for inventory, financials, and invoicing
Build data reconciliation logging
Test each replaced integration against SAP sandbox
Create rollback plan for each procedure replacement
userStories.notes: 13-point story — candidate for splitting into separate stories per SAP module (inventory, financials, invoicing). This is the highest-risk integration work.

userStories.title: Implement API versioning for SAP integration
userStories.user_story: As an IT admin, I want the SAP API integration to support versioning, so that SAP quarterly updates do not break the platform.
acceptance_criteria: Given the API integration uses versioned contracts, when SAP performs a quarterly update, then the existing API version continues to function without changes
Given a new SAP API version is available, when the platform is updated to support it, then the old version remains active during a deprecation period
Given an API version mismatch is detected, when the integration layer processes a request, then it logs a warning and falls back to the compatible version
tasks: Design API versioning strategy (URL path or header-based)
Implement version negotiation in the SAP integration layer
Add version mismatch detection and logging
Document versioning strategy and deprecation policy
Write tests for version fallback scenarios

userStories.title: Build data reconciliation logging for SAP sync
userStories.user_story: As an IT admin, I want a reconciliation log that tracks data discrepancies between SAP and the platform, so that I can identify and resolve sync issues quickly.
acceptance_criteria: Given the platform syncs data with SAP, when a sync cycle completes, then a log entry records the number of records synced, any discrepancies found, and their details
Given a discrepancy is detected, when the admin views the reconciliation log, then the source system, affected record IDs, field-level differences, and timestamps are shown
Given the reconciliation log grows over time, when the admin queries it, then results can be filtered by date range, system, and severity
tasks: Design reconciliation log schema and storage
Implement post-sync comparison logic to detect discrepancies
Build admin UI for viewing and filtering reconciliation logs
Set up automated alerting for high-severity discrepancies
Write tests for discrepancy detection logic

userStories.title: Customer login and work order dashboard
userStories.user_story: As a customer, I want to log into a self-service portal and see a dashboard of my active work orders, so that I can track the status of my service requests without calling the 1-800 number.
acceptance_criteria: Given a customer with valid credentials, when they log into the portal, then they see a dashboard listing all active and recent work orders for their account
Given the dashboard is displayed, when a work order status changes, then the dashboard reflects the update within 2 minutes
Given a customer has no active work orders, when they view the dashboard, then a helpful message indicates no active requests with a link to view history
tasks: Build customer portal web application shell with authentication
Design and implement the work order dashboard layout
Implement API endpoint that returns customer-scoped work orders
Configure Azure AD B2C or equivalent for customer authentication
Write end-to-end tests for login and dashboard display

userStories.title: View real-time work order status details
userStories.user_story: As a customer, I want to see real-time status details for a specific work order, so that I know exactly where my service request stands.
acceptance_criteria: Given a customer clicks on a work order, when the detail page loads, then the current status, assigned technician name, and estimated completion time are displayed
Given a work order is in progress, when the technician updates the status, then the detail page reflects the change within 2 minutes
Given a work order includes milestone events, when the customer views the timeline, then all milestones (created, dispatched, en route, completed) are shown with timestamps
tasks: Build work order detail page with status timeline
Implement real-time status updates via polling or SignalR
Display assigned technician info and ETA
Design responsive layout for desktop and mobile browsers
Write UI tests for detail page components

userStories.title: Customer self-registration flow
userStories.user_story: As a new customer, I want to request portal access through a self-registration form, so that I can start tracking my work orders without calling support.
acceptance_criteria: Given a visitor without an account, when they click Register on the portal, then a self-registration form collects company name, contact info, and account number
Given the form is submitted, when an admin reviews the request, then they can approve or reject the registration with a single click
Given the registration is approved, when the customer is notified, then they receive an email with login instructions
tasks: Build self-registration form with required fields
Create admin review queue for pending registrations
Implement approval/rejection workflow with email notifications
Validate account number against existing customer records
Write tests for registration and approval flows

userStories.title: Send milestone notifications to customers
userStories.user_story: As a customer, I want to receive automated notifications when key milestones are reached on my work orders, so that I stay informed without having to check the portal.
acceptance_criteria: Given a technician is dispatched to a job, when the assignment is confirmed, then the customer receives a notification with the technician name and estimated arrival window
Given a technician marks a job as complete, when the status updates, then the customer receives a completion notification with a summary of work performed
Given the notification infrastructure, when a milestone is triggered, then the notification is delivered within 2 minutes via the customer's preferred channel (email or SMS)
tasks: Implement notification service with email and SMS channels
Define milestone triggers (dispatched, en route, started, completed)
Build notification templates for each milestone type
Integrate with work order status change events
Write integration tests for each milestone notification

userStories.title: Configure customer notification preferences
userStories.user_story: As a customer, I want to choose how I receive notifications (email, SMS, or both), so that I get updates through my preferred communication channel.
acceptance_criteria: Given a customer is logged into the portal, when they navigate to notification settings, then they can select their preferred channel (email, SMS, or both)
Given a customer selects SMS, when future milestone notifications are triggered, then they are sent via SMS instead of email
Given a customer wants to stop notifications, when they select Unsubscribe, then all notifications stop and they can re-enable later
tasks: Build notification preferences settings page in the customer portal
Store preferences per customer account
Route notifications through the preferred channel at send time
Implement unsubscribe and re-subscribe functionality
Write tests for preference changes and routing

userStories.title: Search and filter historical work orders
userStories.user_story: As a customer, I want to search and filter my historical work orders by date, asset, and status, so that I can find past service records quickly.
acceptance_criteria: Given a customer on the portal, when they navigate to the history section, then they can filter work orders by date range, asset, status, and technician
Given a search query is applied, when the customer submits the filter, then matching work orders are displayed within 3 seconds
Given a customer has hundreds of work orders, when they paginate through results, then pages load within 3 seconds each
tasks: Build history search page with filter controls
Implement server-side search and filter API with pagination
Optimize database queries for large datasets (1000+ records per customer)
Write performance tests for search response time
Test with simulated large dataset

userStories.title: Export work order reports
userStories.user_story: As a customer, I want to export my work order history as CSV or PDF, so that I can use the data in my own internal reporting systems.
acceptance_criteria: Given search results are displayed, when the customer clicks Export CSV, then a correctly formatted CSV file downloads with all visible columns
Given search results are displayed, when the customer clicks Export PDF, then a formatted PDF report downloads with Contoso Industrial branding
Given the export contains more than 1000 records, when the customer triggers the export, then it completes within 10 seconds and the file is delivered as a download
tasks: Implement CSV export with all work order fields
Implement PDF export with report layout and branding
Add export buttons to the work order history page
Handle large export datasets with server-side generation
Write tests for CSV and PDF output correctness

userStories.title: Display real-time job status metrics on dispatch dashboard
userStories.user_story: As a dispatcher, I want to see real-time counts of active, completed, and unassigned work orders on my dashboard, so that I can monitor daily operations at a glance.
acceptance_criteria: Given a dispatcher opens the operational dashboard, when the page loads, then they see tile counts for active, completed, unassigned, and overdue work orders for the current day
Given work orders are being completed in the field, when the dashboard auto-refreshes, then metrics update within 60 seconds
Given the dispatcher selects a different date, when the filter is applied, then all metrics recalculate for the selected date
tasks: Design dashboard layout with KPI tiles
Build real-time data aggregation service for work order metrics
Implement auto-refresh at configurable intervals (default 60 seconds)
Add date filter for historical day views
Write tests for metric calculation accuracy
userStories.notes: Dashboard must load within 3 seconds per feature DoD.

userStories.title: Drill down from dashboard metrics to work order lists
userStories.user_story: As a dispatcher, I want to click a metric tile and see the filtered list of work orders behind it, so that I can quickly investigate specific categories.
acceptance_criteria: Given a dispatcher views the dashboard, when they click the Overdue tile, then they see a filtered list of all overdue work orders with details
Given the filtered list is displayed, when the dispatcher clicks a work order, then they navigate to the full work order detail view
Given the dispatcher drills down, when they click the back button, then they return to the dashboard with preserved filter state
tasks: Implement click handlers on dashboard tiles to navigate to filtered views
Build filtered work order list page with consistent columns
Preserve navigation state for back-button support
Add export option for filtered results
Write UI tests for drill-down navigation

userStories.title: Display executive KPIs with time range filters
userStories.user_story: As an operations executive, I want to view key performance indicators like average time-to-resolution and first-time fix rate over configurable time periods, so that I can measure operational performance.
acceptance_criteria: Given an executive opens the KPI dashboard, when the page loads, then they see average time-to-resolution, first-time fix rate, technician utilization, and customer satisfaction for the default time period (this month)
Given the executive selects a different time range, when the filter is applied, then all KPIs recalculate and display within 5 seconds
Given the time range filter options, when the executive views them, then options include: this week, this month, this quarter, and custom date range
tasks: Design executive KPI dashboard layout
Implement KPI calculation queries (time-to-resolution, first-time fix rate, utilization)
Build time range filter component with presets and custom range
Optimize query performance for large datasets within 5-second target
Write tests validating KPI accuracy against manual calculations
userStories.notes: KPI accuracy should be validated against manual calculations per feature DoD.

userStories.title: Show trend charts for executive KPIs
userStories.user_story: As an operations executive, I want to see week-over-week and month-over-month trend charts for key metrics, so that I can identify performance improvements or regressions.
acceptance_criteria: Given KPI data is available, when the executive views the dashboard, then trend charts show week-over-week comparisons for all primary KPIs
Given the executive switches to a monthly view, when the chart updates, then month-over-month trends are displayed as line or bar charts
Given a trend line shows a significant change, when the executive hovers over a data point, then a tooltip shows the exact value and the percentage change from the prior period
tasks: Select and integrate charting library (e.g., Chart.js, Recharts)
Build trend visualization components for line and bar charts
Implement week-over-week and month-over-month data aggregation
Add tooltips with exact values and percentage changes
Test chart rendering on desktop and tablet

userStories.title: Display platform component health status
userStories.user_story: As an IT admin, I want to see the health status of all platform components and integrations on a single dashboard, so that I can detect and respond to issues quickly.
acceptance_criteria: Given an IT admin opens the health dashboard, when the page loads, then they see current availability status (healthy/degraded/down) for all platform components and integrations (SAP, Maximo, FleetComplete, Azure AD)
Given an integration endpoint is down, when the health check detects the failure, then the component status changes to red within 5 minutes
Given historical health data, when the admin views the 30-day timeline, then uptime/downtime periods are displayed as a color-coded timeline bar
tasks: Implement health check probes for all critical endpoints
Design health dashboard layout with component status cards
Build 30-day health history timeline visualization
Store health check results for historical analysis
Write tests for health check detection and status transitions

userStories.title: Configure automated health alerts
userStories.user_story: As an IT admin, I want to receive automated alerts when a platform component goes down or becomes degraded, so that I can respond to issues before users are impacted.
acceptance_criteria: Given an integration endpoint goes down, when the health check detects the failure, then an alert is sent to the IT team via email and Teams within 5 minutes
Given the alert is sent, when the admin views it, then it includes the component name, failure type, timestamp, and a link to the health dashboard
Given the component recovers, when the health check detects recovery, then a resolution notification is sent indicating the issue is resolved
tasks: Implement alerting engine triggered by health check state changes
Configure email and Teams notification channels for alerts
Build alert templates with component details and dashboard links
Implement recovery detection and resolution notifications
Create runbook for common alert responses
userStories.notes: Runbook creation is part of the feature's definition of done.

userStories.title: Implement Azure AD SSO for all platform modules
userStories.user_story: As a platform user, I want to sign in once using my Contoso Azure AD credentials and access all platform modules without re-authenticating, so that my login experience is seamless.
acceptance_criteria: Given a user with a valid Contoso Azure AD account, when they navigate to any platform module (web portal, mobile app, admin), then they are authenticated via SSO without entering separate credentials
Given a user is not in the Contoso Azure AD tenant, when they attempt to access the platform, then access is denied with a clear error message
Given MFA is enabled in Azure AD, when a user authenticates, then the MFA challenge is presented and the platform proceeds after successful verification
tasks: Register the platform application in Contoso Azure AD tenant
Implement MSAL authentication in the web portal
Implement MSAL authentication in the mobile app
Configure redirect URIs and scopes for each platform module
Write end-to-end tests for SSO flow across modules

userStories.title: Handle session expiry and token refresh
userStories.user_story: As a platform user, I want my session to remain active while I am working and transparently refresh, so that I am not unexpectedly logged out during field work.
acceptance_criteria: Given a user's access token is about to expire, when the token refresh runs, then a new token is obtained silently without user interaction
Given the refresh token has expired, when the user next interacts with the platform, then they are redirected to Azure AD login and returned to their original page after re-authentication
Given the user is on the mobile app offline, when they come back online with an expired session, then the app handles re-authentication gracefully without losing unsaved work
tasks: Implement silent token refresh using MSAL token cache
Handle refresh token expiry with graceful redirect to login
Preserve navigation state across re-authentication
Test token refresh on mobile during offline-to-online transitions
Write unit tests for token lifecycle management

userStories.title: Configure predefined RBAC roles
userStories.user_story: As an IT admin, I want to configure predefined roles (Technician, Dispatcher, Supervisor, Finance, Account Manager, IT Admin), so that each user only sees the data and features appropriate to their job function.
acceptance_criteria: Given the six predefined roles, when an IT admin views the role management screen, then each role is listed with its associated permissions for data access and feature access
Given a user with the Technician role, when they access the platform, then they can only see their own assigned work orders and cannot access finance or admin modules
Given a user with the Finance role, when they access the platform, then they can see the approval queue and cost details but cannot modify dispatch schedules
tasks: Define permission matrix for all six roles across all platform features
Implement role-based authorization middleware in the API layer
Build admin UI for viewing role definitions and permissions
Apply role checks to all API endpoints and UI components
Write tests for each role's access boundaries

userStories.title: Sync role assignments with Azure AD groups
userStories.user_story: As an IT admin, I want user role assignments to sync with Azure AD security groups, so that I can manage access through our existing identity infrastructure.
acceptance_criteria: Given an IT admin adds a user to the Dispatcher Azure AD security group, when the user next logs in, then they automatically receive the Dispatcher role permissions
Given a user is removed from an Azure AD group, when the role sync runs, then their platform role is revoked within 15 minutes
Given an IT admin views the admin panel, when they check role assignments, then the list shows which Azure AD group each user's role is derived from
tasks: Map Azure AD security groups to platform roles
Implement group membership sync via Microsoft Graph API
Build admin panel showing role-to-group mappings
Handle group membership caching with periodic refresh
Write integration tests for sync scenarios

userStories.title: Restrict sensitive contract data by role
userStories.user_story: As a security admin, I want sensitive customer contract data hidden from users below Account Manager level, so that confidential pricing and terms are protected.
acceptance_criteria: Given a user without Account Manager or higher role, when they view a customer profile, then contract details (pricing, terms, SLAs) are hidden or masked
Given an Account Manager, when they view a customer profile, then all contract details including pricing and terms are fully visible
Given a Technician attempts to access a contract data API endpoint directly, when the request is processed, then it returns a 403 Forbidden response
tasks: Classify all data fields with sensitivity labels (public, internal, confidential)
Implement field-level filtering in API responses based on caller's role
Update UI components to conditionally render sensitive fields
Add API-level authorization checks for contract data endpoints
Write penetration tests for role boundary enforcement

userStories.title: Log all access attempts to restricted data
userStories.user_story: As an IT admin, I want all access attempts to sensitive data logged with full audit details, so that I can investigate potential unauthorized access.
acceptance_criteria: Given any user attempts to access restricted data, when the request is processed, then an audit log entry records user ID, timestamp, resource accessed, and outcome (allowed or denied)
Given an admin queries the audit log, when they filter by date range or user, then matching entries are returned within 5 seconds
Given a denied access attempt, when it occurs, then the attempt is flagged as a potential security event and included in the daily security report
tasks: Implement audit logging middleware for restricted data endpoints
Store audit logs in a tamper-proof append-only store
Build audit log query UI for IT admins
Implement daily security report aggregating denied access attempts
Write tests for audit log completeness and accuracy

userStories.title: Store compliance documents with encryption and metadata
userStories.user_story: As a field technician, I want my compliance photos, signatures, and checklists stored securely with metadata tagging, so that they are protected and traceable to the originating work order.
acceptance_criteria: Given a technician submits a work order with compliance documents, when the documents are stored, then each is encrypted at rest and tagged with work order ID, timestamp, and technician ID
Given a compliance document is uploaded, when it is saved, then it is immutable and cannot be modified or deleted by non-admin users
Given a stored document, when an admin retrieves it, then the metadata (work order, technician, timestamp) is available alongside the document
tasks: Configure Azure Blob Storage with encryption at rest in US region
Implement metadata tagging on upload (work order ID, technician ID, timestamp, document type)
Set immutability policies to prevent modification or deletion
Build document upload service handling photos up to 20MB
Write integration tests for upload, metadata, and immutability

userStories.title: Enforce US data residency for compliance storage
userStories.user_story: As an IT admin, I want all compliance documents stored exclusively in US Azure regions, so that we meet our data residency requirements for regulated customers.
acceptance_criteria: Given compliance documents are stored, when the storage account location is audited, then it is confirmed to be in a US Azure region (e.g., East US, West US)
Given a deployment attempt to a non-US region, when Azure Policy evaluates the deployment, then it is denied and the violation is logged
Given an annual compliance audit, when the auditor reviews storage configuration, then documented evidence of US-only residency is available
tasks: Verify Azure Blob Storage account is provisioned in a US region
Implement Azure Policy to deny non-US storage deployments
Document data residency configuration for compliance audits
Write automated compliance check tests

userStories.title: Configure seven-year document retention policies
userStories.user_story: As an IT admin, I want automated retention policies ensuring compliance documents are kept for a minimum of seven years, so that we meet regulatory retention mandates.
acceptance_criteria: Given a compliance document stored in the system, when the retention period is checked, then it is set to a minimum of seven years from the creation date
Given a document has reached its retention expiry, when the lifecycle policy runs, then it is archived (not deleted) and an admin is notified
Given an admin queries retention status, when they view the report, then they see how many documents are active, approaching expiry, and expired

userStories.title: Implement searchable audit trail for compliance records
userStories.user_story: As an IT admin, I want a searchable audit trail tracking who created, accessed, or modified compliance records, so that we can demonstrate full traceability during audits.
acceptance_criteria: Given any user accesses a compliance document, when the access occurs, then an audit log entry is created with user ID, timestamp, document ID, and action type (viewed, downloaded)
Given an IT admin queries the audit trail, when they search by work order or date range, then all access and modification events are returned within 5 seconds

Store audit logs in tamper-proof append-only storage (Azure Table Storage or Log Analytics)
Build audit trail search UI with work order and date range filters
Ensure audit logs are retainable for the same 7-year retention period

userStories.title: Send adaptive card notifications in Teams
userStories.user_story: As a field technician, I want to receive work order notifications as adaptive cards in Microsoft Teams, so that I can see assignment details directly in the app I already use.
acceptance_criteria: Given a technician is assigned a new work order, when the assignment is saved, then they receive a Teams message with an adaptive card showing work order summary (customer, location, priority, scheduled time)

userStories.title: Deliver approval request notifications via Teams
userStories.user_story: As a supervisor, I want to receive approval request notifications in Teams with approve/reject action buttons, so that I can act on work orders without switching to the portal.

userStories.title: View work order details in Teams task module
userStories.user_story: As a field technician, I want to view full work order details directly in a Teams task module, so that I can get all the information I need without switching apps.
acceptance_criteria: Given a technician receives a work order notification in Teams, when they click View Details, then a Teams task module opens showing the full work order with customer, asset, and task information

userStories.title: Update work order status from within Teams
userStories.user_story: As a field technician, I want to accept, flag, or mark myself en route directly from Teams, so that I can update my status without opening the dedicated mobile app.
acceptance_criteria: Given a technician viewing a work order in Teams, when they tap Accept, then the work order status updates in the platform in real time and the card refreshes
Given a technician viewing a work order in Teams, when they tap Mark En Route, then the status updates and the customer is notified of the technician's departure

userStories.title: Provision Azure infrastructure with IaC
userStories.user_story: As an IT admin, I want the Azure hosting infrastructure defined and provisioned using Infrastructure as Code, so that environments are reproducible and version-controlled.
acceptance_criteria: Given the infrastructure requirements, when the IaC templates are executed, then all required Azure resources (App Service, SQL, Storage, etc.) are provisioned in US Azure regions

userStories.title: Enforce US data residency via Azure Policy
userStories.user_story: As an IT admin, I want Azure Policy to enforce that all resources hosting customer data are deployed only in US regions, so that data residency requirements are guaranteed at the platform level.
acceptance_criteria: Given an Azure Policy is assigned to the subscription, when any resource deployment targets a non-US region, then the deployment is denied and the violation is logged
Given the policy is active, when an admin views the compliance dashboard, then they see 100% compliance for resource location constraints
Given a new resource is deployed to a US region, when the policy evaluates, then the deployment is allowed without delay
